subject: correspondence
evidence: Correspondence should be addressed to: Secretary, B16 Standards Committee

subject: requests for cases
object: statement of need and background information
evidence: Requests for Cases shall provide a Statement of Need and Background Information.

subject: b16 committee
evidence: Upon request, the B16 Committee will render an interpretation of any requirement of the Standard.

subject: cases
evidence: Cases may be issued for the purpose of providing alternative rules when justified

subject: cases
evidence: Cases are effective immediately upon ASME approval

subject: thread gaging notch
evidence: The gaging notch of the working gage shall come flush with the bottom of the chamfer in all threaded flanges

subject: depth of chamfer
object: one-half the pitch of the thread
evidence: This depth of chamfer is approximately equal to one-half the pitch of the thread

subject: maximum allowable thread variation
evidence: The maximum allowable thread variation is one turn large or small from the gaging notch

subject: annex a
object: distance and number of turns for external pipe threads
evidence: Annex A specifies the distance and number of turns that external pipe threads may be made longer than regular for use with the higher pressure flanges

subject: stud bolts
evidence: Stud bolts, threaded at both ends or threaded full length, or bolts may be used in flange joints

subject: table 1c
object: dimensional recommendations for bolts, stud bolts, and nuts
evidence: Dimensional recommendations for bolts, stud bolts, and nuts are shown in Table 1C

subject: stud bolt length L
object: height of two heavy hexagon nuts
evidence: The tabulated stud bolt length L does not include the height of end points

subject: end point
object: unthreaded length extending beyond the thread
evidence: An end point is defined as an unthreaded length, such as a chamfer, which extends beyond the thread

subject: table_2-3.16
evidence: Table 2-3.16 Pressure–Temperature Ratings for Group 3.16 Materials

subject: n08810
evidence: N08810 (1) Working Pressures by Classes, bar

subject: 35ni–19cr–11⁄4si
evidence: 35Ni–19Cr–11⁄4Si B536 Gr. N08330 (1) Working Pressures by Classes, bar

evidence: Table II-2-2.12 Pressure–Temperature Ratings for Group 2.12 Materials

subject: table_ii-2-3-1
evidence: Table II-2-3.1 Pressure–Temperature Ratings for Group 3.1 Materials

evidence: Table II-2-3.2 Pressure–Temperature Ratings for Group 3.2 Material

subject: group_2_12_materials
evidence: At temperatures over 1,000°F, use only when the carbon content is 0.04% or higher.

subject: group_3_1_materials
evidence: Use annealed material only.

subject: B564 Gr. N06600 (1)
evidence: Table II-2-3.5 Pressure–Temperature Ratings for Group 3.5 Materials

evidence: Table II-2-3.6 Pressure–Temperature Ratings for Group 3.6 Materials

subject: 46Fe–24Ni–21Cr–6Mo–Cu–N
evidence: 46Fe–24Ni–21Cr–6Mo–Cu–N B462 Gr. A351

subject: ratings
evidence: Ratings for flanges and flanged fittings terminate at 538°C (1,000°F).

subject: ratings of flanges and flanged fittings
evidence: Ratings of flanges and flanged fittings terminate at 1,000°F (538°C).

subject: gasket materials and construction
evidence: Classification of gasket materials and types is shown in Table B-1.

subject: gasket materials and construction
evidence: Reference to a dimensional standard for gaskets (e.g., ASME B16.21) is recommended.

subject: gasket dimensions
evidence: selected dimensions should be based on the type of gasket and its characteristics.

subject: gasket characteristics
evidence: These characteristics include density, flexibility, compatibility with the fluid being contained, and gasket compression needed to maintain sealing.
